items.title: US Open +15m
items.summary: US Open +15m shows mild risk-off conditions. S&P -0.50%, NDQ -0.13%, 31% of tracked names positive, led by NOVO-B.CO, AMD, DUOL.

alpha: - Session leans risk-off: S&P -0.50%, NDQ -0.13%. Breadth is soft (6 positive vs 13 negative) but selling pressure remains orderly — no signs of forced liquidation.
- Leadership quality is mixed; NOVO-B.CO shows relative strength but no single sector is driving the session with conviction.
- Tilt defensively: trim high-beta positions, avoid forcing new longs into weakness. Let the tape show stabilization before re-engaging.
beta: - Top movers: **NOVO-B.CO (+2.55%)**, **AMD (+1.87%)**, **DUOL (+1.19%)**. No extreme outliers — moves reflect broad tape direction rather than isolated catalysts.
- S&P opening weak (-0.50%). Avoid buying the first dip — let the tape stabilize into the 10am window before re-engaging.
- Manage size on laggards: **RDDT (-3.60%)**, **BA (-3.38%)**, **MA (-2.09%)** are showing weakness — cut or hedge rather than averaging down against the tape.
pulse: Awaiting pulse update.

items.title: US Open +15m
items.summary: US open shows modest index strength and positive breadth among tracked names, led by BTC, TM, and LMT with no major downside dislocations.

alpha: - The tape remains constructive: broad downside stress is absent, and index structure is stable-to-firm at the open.
- Leadership is rotating through cyclicals/quality (TM, LMT, payments) while mega-cap tech is mostly flat, supporting a selective-risk framework.
- Keep strategic exposure centered on liquid leaders; treat late/previous-session prints (e.g., GOOG/BABA/LMT/TM/V/MA/NOVO-B.CO timestamps) with lower signal weight intraday.
beta: - If S&P/NDQ hold opening range into the first hour, allow incremental adds in names showing live relative strength (TM, LMT, BTC proxy risk sleeve).
- For near-flat mega-caps (AAPL/MSFT/NVDA/AMZN), prefer breakout-or-retest entries rather than market buys in the middle of the range.
- Keep stops tight and size smaller in names with thin early volume or stale timestamps; avoid forcing trades where the print quality is mixed.
pulse: Awaiting pulse update.

items.title: EU/Nordic Morning
items.summary: US-led upside breadth carries into Europe; software/high-beta lead while downside is shallow and concentrated.

alpha: - Breadth and sector mix support maintaining core long exposure rather than defensive rotation.
- Leadership is not only in megacaps: software/media and industrial cyclicals are contributing, which lowers concentration risk.
- Keep macro humility: missing US10Y/DXY/VIX means sentiment read is price-led, not fully cross-confirmed.
beta: - Prioritize continuation watchlist: **ADBE, DUOL, NFLX, TSLA, BA** (current top movers).
- Enter via pullbacks/holds above intraday support; avoid adding at local extension after >3% pops.
- Cap single-name risk where catalyst check is flagged (ADBE, DUOL, NFLX, TSLA) until independently verified.

- Dispersion favors stock selection over directional index aggression.

- Keep tighter stops than usual; today’s tape can rotate fast between “calm index” and “single-name stress.”
